title: Mid-Level Harness Blocks — graphLens, resilientPipeline, guardedExecution
items: graphLens() factory shipped in src/patterns/lens.ts. Exposes reactive observability surfaces: stats: Node<TopologyStats> (nodeCount, edgeCount, subgraphCount, sources, sinks, depth, hasCycles) recomputed on structural change via watchTopologyTree; health: Node<HealthReport> ({ok, problems: [{path, status, upstreamCause?}]}) — V1 reports 'errored' status only with upstream-cause walkback via reachable(); flow: ReactiveMapBundle<string, FlowEntry> — per-path DATA counts + lastUpdate_ns, backed by reactiveMap (lazy snapshot materialization, O(1) get/has/size, maxFlowPaths LRU cap); why(from, to) method returns live Node<CausalChain> via reactiveExplainPath. Named 'stats' (not 'topology') to avoid collision with inherited Graph.topology event stream. Single consolidated watchTopologyTree + single observe() subscription dispatched to all three surfaces.

resilientPipeline() factory shipped in src/patterns/resilient-pipeline.ts. Canonical order: rateLimit → budget → breaker → timeout → retry → fallback → withStatus. Timeout wraps source BEFORE retry so each retry attempt resubscribes to a fresh deadline (per-attempt semantics). All layers optional. Bundle: {node, status, error, breakerState?}. Subsumes the pre-1.0 resilientFetch template. timeoutMs bounded ≤ 9_000_000 to stay within Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER ns arithmetic.

guardedExecution() factory shipped in src/patterns/guarded-execution.ts. GuardedExecutionGraph extends Graph, wraps policyEnforcer (enforce mode default, now with full transitive dynamic coverage via watchTopologyTree). scopedDescribe(opts?) method delegates to target.describe({actor}) with default-actor injection and per-call override. Exposes violations: TopicGraph<PolicyViolation> at top level for composition with graphLens.health. V1 scope: policies + actor + scoped describe; budget option deferred (users layer a budget-aware PolicyRuleData into policies list).

persistentState() retired — superseded by Graph.attachStorage() (shipped in graph-module 24-unit review wave). attachStorage provides multi-tier storage with per-tier debounce, autoRestore option, diff-mode via compactEvery rotation, tier-≥3 gating, scoped paths, and a suite of StorageTier implementations (memory/dict/file/sqlite/indexedDb). Manual save/restore/diff/list/delete shipped as §9.3-core surface ops. One-call equivalent: graph.attachStorage([sqliteStorage(path)], {autoRestore: true}). No further work required.

Helper watchTopologyTree(graph, cb) shipped in src/graph/topology-tree.ts. Transitive structural-change subscription — auto-wires new mounts, disposes on unmount via qualified-prefix matching (robust to deep descendants whose _parent pointers don't null on unmount). Callback signature (event, emitter, prefix) lets consumers compute qualified paths without walking ancestors. Used by graphLens (stats/health/flow recompute triggers + flow cleanup on node/mount removal) and by policyEnforcer (full transitive dynamic coverage). Exported from graph/index.ts.

Tests: 15 topology-companion + 13 lens (including flow reactiveMap variants, maxFlowPaths LRU, qualified paths, mount-removal subtree cleanup) + 9 resilientPipeline + 8 guardedExecution + 6 policyEnforcer dynamic-coverage regression cases. Total +51 new tests in this wave.
